narrative: I correctly diagnosed the fatigue driver as recurring 2-4am checkout/payments/inventory alerts concentrated on alex (66.7% recurrence, 73.8% owner share), and my signals tracked that snapshot. However my purpose/signals are overfitted: hard-coding alex, 3 components, and a fixed 02-04 UTC window risks noise-shifting blind spots, and the owner count-down signal encourages whack-a-mole vs fair distribution. I lack alert quality/triage signals (silencing is only 7.1%, no MTTR/flap dampening), and my recurrence/night signals are siloed missing their intersection – the actual sleep disruption driver. Memory is quantitative but has no root-cause hypothesis (nightly batch) and no brittleness note.

proposals.rationale: Current purpose hard-codes alex + checkout/payments/inventory + 02-04am – brittle to noise migration and owner churn. Broaden to durable noise/fatigue reduction with balanced ownership.

payload.statement: Reduce actionable alert noise and flapping for BrewBean POS SRE, protecting overnight on-call focus time through balanced ownership and sustainable triage, not owner/component-specific whack-a-mole.
payload.rationale: Preserves overnight/fatigue intent while removing snapshot overfitting to alex and 3 components

proposals.rationale: count(owner='alex') down can pass while Priya becomes the new bottleneck. Track distribution fairness to protect on-call health.

payload.extraction: max(count by owner) / count(alerts) per day
payload.rationale: Prevents shifting load from alex to priya; measures on-call concentration risk vs single-owner count

proposals.rationale: Low 7.1% silencing with 66.7% recurrence indicates missing flap dampening/triage hygiene. Volume-only signals miss quality.

payload.rationale: Tracks appropriate suppression/triage maturity for flapping overnight alerts, complementing pure volume reduction

proposals.rationale: Current leading signals track recurrence and 02-04am separately, missing the core fatigue intersection that drives sleep disruption.

payload.extraction: count(recurring=true AND timestamp hour between 02:00-04:00 UTC) / count(alerts) per day
payload.rationale: Direct proxy for overnight flap fatigue – the actual on-call focus killer, stronger than either signal alone

proposals.rationale: Memory is pure counts with no root cause hypothesis or metric brittleness warning, leading to owner-whack-a-mole recommendations.

payload.content: 2-4am checkout/payments/inventory flaps correlate with nightly inventory sync/batch jobs – investigate flap dampening / SLO burn thresholds before owner rebalancing. Owner concentration metric (alex@brewbean.example, 73.8%) is snapshot-brittle – track max owner share for fairness, not a named individual.

proposals.rationale: Component signal only watches checkout/payments/inventory, allowing noise migration to go undetected while north-star stays flat.

payload.scenario: Noise migrates from checkout/payments/inventory to auth/catalog, keeping total alerts/day flat – current component signal gives false green

proposals.rationale: Single-owner count signal is gameable; need test for load-shifting failure mode.

payload.scenario: Alex alert count drops to 5/day but Priya/ops absorb load, max owner share stays >70% – owner='alex' signal passes while on-call bottleneck persists

proposals.rationale: Fixed 02-04 UTC window is brittle to schedule drift / DST / job slippage.

payload.scenario: Overnight alerts shift to 04-06am UTC due to batch job slippage, preserving sleep disruption – fixed 02-04am signal misses fatigue regression
